heading: Solar Fuels Research Initiative
label: 16313 Solar Fuels Research Initiative

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out a research initiative, to be known as the “Solar Fuels Research Initiative” (referred to in this section as the “Initiative”) to expand theoretical and fundamental knowledge of photochemistry, electrochemistry, biochemistry, and materials science useful for the practical development of experimental systems to convert solar energy to chemical energy.

children.label: A
children.text: the Basic Energy Sciences Program and the Biological and Environmental Research Program of the Office of Science; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Office of Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In carrying out the Initiative, the Secretary shall organize activities among multidisciplinary teams to leverage, to the maximum extent practicable, expertise from the National Laboratories, institutions of higher education, and the private sector.

children.heading: Goals
children.label: B Goals
children.text: The multidisciplinary teams described in subparagraph (A) shall pursue aggressive, milestone-driven, basic research goals.

children.heading: Resources
children.label: C Resources
children.text: The Secretary shall provide sufficient resources to the multidisciplinary teams described in subparagraph (A) to achieve the goals described in subparagraph (B) over a period of time to be determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Additional activities
children.label: 4 Additional activities
children.text: The Secretary may organize additional activities under this subsection through Energy Frontier Research Centers, Energy Innovation Hubs, or other organizational structures.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out under the Initiative a program to support research needed to bridge scientific barriers to, and discover knowledge relevant to, artificial photosynthetic systems.

children.label: i
children.text: photoinduced production of hydrogen and oxygen from water; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the sustainable photoinduced reduction of carbon dioxide to fuel products including hydrocarbons, alcohols, carbon monoxide, and natural gas; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Assistant Secretary for Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy shall support translational research, development, and validation of physical concepts developed under the program.

children.heading: Standard of review
children.label: 3 Standard of review
children.text: The Secretary shall review activities carried out under the program described in paragraph (1) to determine the achievement of technical milestones.

children.heading: Funds
children.label: 4 Funds
children.text: Of the funds authorized to be appropriated for basic energy sciences in a fiscal year, there is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out activities under this subsection $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

children.heading: Biochemistry, replication of natural photosynthesis, and related processes
children.label: c Biochemistry, replication of natural photosynthesis, and related processes

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall carry out under the Initiative a program to support research needed to replicate natural photosynthetic processes by use of artificial photosynthetic components and materials.

children.label: i
children.text: the photoinduced reduction of dinitrogen to ammonia;

children.label: ii
children.text: the absorption of carbon dioxide from ambient air;

children.label: iii
children.text: molecular-based charge separation and storage;

children.label: iv
children.text: photoinitiated electron transfer; and

children.label: v
children.text: catalysis in biological or biomimetic systems;

children.label: B
children.text: the Associate Director of Biological and Environmental Research shall support systems biology and genomics approaches to understand genetic and physiological pathways connected to photosynthetic mechanisms; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Assistant Secretary for Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy shall support translational research, development, and validation of physical concepts developed under the program.

children.heading: Standard of review
children.label: 3 Standard of review
children.text: The Secretary shall review activities carried out under the program described in paragraph (1) to determine the achievement of technical milestones.

children.heading: Funds
children.label: 4 Funds
children.text: Of the funds authorized to be appropriated for basic energy sciences in a fiscal year, there is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out activities under this subsection $50,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.
